question_text: Theileriosis (tropical theileriosis) of cattle is caused by which group of microscopic parasitic organisms in India?
explanation: Tropical theileriosis is caused by Theileria annulata, a protozoan parasite of the phylum Apicomplexa. It is transmitted by the bite of Hyalomma ticks, which inject sporozoites that invade lymphocytes and red cells. Common signs are high fever, swollen lymph nodes and severe anaemia, often fatal in cross-bred cattle without timely treatment.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: A spore-forming bacterium of the genus Bacillus
A protozoan parasite of the phylum Apicomplexa
A double-stranded DNA virus of the family Herpesviridae
A parasitic flatworm of the class Trematoda

elimination_hints.hint: Bacillus species cause anthrax, not theileriosis
Theileriosis is not viral
Trematodes cause fascioliasis, not theileriosis

question_text: Ranikhet disease (Newcastle disease) is a highly contagious disease that primarily affects which specific category of livestock?
explanation: Ranikhet disease, also internationally known as Newcastle disease, is caused by Newcastle Disease Virus, a paramyxovirus. It primarily affects domestic poultry, especially chickens, with high mortality. Clinical signs include respiratory distress, greenish diarrhoea and nervous symptoms like twisted neck (torticollis). Vaccination with F1 or LaSota strains is the main control measure.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Adult dairy cows in lactation only
Goats and sheep grazing in arid regions
Domestic poultry, especially chickens of all ages
Buffalo calves under six months of age

elimination_hints.hint: Ranikhet does not affect cattle clinically
Ranikhet is not a small ruminant disease
Buffalo calves are not affected by Newcastle disease

question_text: Surra disease in horses, camels and cattle is caused by which specific blood-dwelling protozoan parasite of livestock?
explanation: Surra is caused by Trypanosoma evansi, a flagellate protozoan that lives in the blood plasma of infected animals. It is mechanically transmitted by biting flies of the genus Tabanus. Clinical signs include intermittent fever, progressive anaemia, weight loss and oedema. It is particularly serious in camels and horses, often fatal without treatment.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Babesia bigemina transmitted by Boophilus ticks
Trypanosoma evansi transmitted by Tabanid biting flies
Theileria annulata transmitted by Hyalomma ticks
Anaplasma marginale transmitted by Dermacentor ticks

elimination_hints.hint: Babesia causes red-water (babesiosis), not Surra
Theileria causes theileriosis, not Surra
Anaplasma causes anaplasmosis, not Surra

question_text: Fascioliasis or liver-fluke disease of cattle and sheep requires which specific intermediate host to complete its life cycle?
explanation: Fasciola gigantica and Fasciola hepatica are liver flukes whose life cycle requires a freshwater snail (Lymnaea species) as intermediate host. Eggs from infected animals hatch in water into miracidia that infect snails, where they multiply and are released as cercariae. Cercariae encyst as metacercariae on grass, and grazing animals get infected by ingesting them.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: A blood-sucking tick of the genus Boophilus
A biting fly belonging to the family Tabanidae
A freshwater snail of the genus Lymnaea
A mosquito of the genus Anopheles in marshes

elimination_hints.hint: Ticks are vectors of protozoan blood parasites, not flukes
Tabanid flies transmit Surra, not flukes
Anopheles transmits malaria parasites, not liver fluke

question_text: Marek's disease in poultry is caused by which specific class of virus and primarily affects which body system of chicks?
explanation: Marek's disease is caused by Gallid herpesvirus 2, an oncogenic herpesvirus, and primarily affects the nervous system of young chicks, leading to leg paralysis, drooping wings, and visceral lymphoid tumours. It is controlled by vaccination of day-old chicks at the hatchery using turkey herpesvirus (HVT) and related vaccines.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Coronavirus affecting only the respiratory tract of layers
Herpesvirus affecting nervous system causing leg paralysis
Bacterium of genus Salmonella causing severe diarrhoea
Protozoan Eimeria affecting the intestine of broilers

elimination_hints.hint: Coronavirus causes infectious bronchitis, not Marek's
Salmonella causes pullorum or fowl typhoid
Eimeria causes coccidiosis, not nervous-system disease

question_text: Infectious Bursal Disease (Gumboro) in poultry primarily damages which specific lymphoid organ in young growing chicks?
explanation: Infectious Bursal Disease is caused by IBD virus (Birnaviridae) and primarily destroys lymphocytes in the bursa of Fabricius of young chicks of three to six weeks. Damage to the bursa causes severe immunosuppression, making birds prone to other infections and leading to vaccine failures. Hence good IBD vaccination is critical for poultry health.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Spleen of adult laying hens above one year
Thymus of newly hatched chicks of one day
Bursa of Fabricius of chicks of three to six weeks
Liver and kidneys of older breeder hens only

elimination_hints.hint: IBD targets bursa, not spleen
IBD affects bursa more than thymus
IBD does not primarily damage liver or kidneys

question_text: Coccidiosis in poultry is caused by which specific protozoan parasite that primarily damages which organ of the bird?
explanation: Poultry coccidiosis is caused by various species of the protozoan genus Eimeria, which invade and destroy intestinal epithelial cells. It causes bloody diarrhoea, weight loss, reduced feed conversion and high mortality, especially in growing broilers. It is controlled by anticoccidial drugs in feed (coccidiostats) and live attenuated coccidiosis vaccines.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Salmonella bacterium primarily affecting the spleen
Eimeria protozoan species primarily affecting the intestine
Aspergillus fungus primarily affecting the lungs
Reovirus primarily affecting the joints of broilers

elimination_hints.hint: Salmonella causes pullorum, not coccidiosis
Aspergillus causes brooder pneumonia, not coccidiosis
Reovirus causes viral arthritis, not gut disease

question_text: All-in all-out management practice in poultry farming is primarily adopted to achieve which specific biosecurity benefit?
explanation: The all-in all-out system means all birds enter the shed on the same day and are removed on the same day, after which the shed is cleaned, disinfected and rested before the next batch. This breaks disease transmission cycles between batches, especially of viral diseases like Ranikhet, IBD and Marek's, and is a core poultry biosecurity practice.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: It increases egg production by mixing different ages of birds
It improves uniform feeding of birds across multiple ages
It breaks disease transmission cycles between successive batches
It reduces the cost of buying day-old chicks dramatically

elimination_hints.hint: Mixing ages actually worsens disease spread
Feeding uniformity is incidental, not the main purpose
Cost of chicks is not the driver for all-in all-out

question_text: Which of the following diseases is correctly classified as a notifiable zoonotic disease that legally must be reported to authorities?
explanation: Anthrax is a notifiable zoonosis worldwide and in India under the Prevention and Control of Infectious and Contagious Diseases in Animals Act 2009. Any suspicion of anthrax in livestock must be reported immediately to the State Veterinary Department because of its severe public-health implications and the potential for spore contamination of pastures.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Mild keratoconjunctivitis in adult cattle herds in India
Foot rot in growing sheep flocks during the monsoon
Anthrax in cattle, a serious notifiable zoonotic disease
Common cold of poultry birds during the cold winter only

elimination_hints.hint: Mild eye infections are not notifiable zoonoses
Foot rot is locally important but not a notifiable zoonosis
Mild colds in poultry are not notifiable

question_text: In a poultry farm with sudden high mortality and twisted necks in chicks, which disease should be the most likely first suspicion?
explanation: Sudden high mortality with respiratory distress, greenish diarrhoea and nervous signs such as torticollis (twisted neck) and paralysis is the classical clinical picture of Ranikhet disease (Newcastle disease) in unvaccinated or poorly vaccinated flocks. Confirmation is by virus isolation or HI test, but field treatment focuses on biosecurity and emergency vaccination of unaffected sheds.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Coccidiosis with bloody diarrhoea in young broilers
Ranikhet disease with respiratory and nervous signs
Aspergillosis in chicks of two weeks of age only
Vitamin A deficiency causing dryness of the eyes alone

elimination_hints.hint: Coccidiosis causes bloody droppings, not twisted necks
Aspergillosis causes pneumonia, not nervous signs
Vitamin A deficiency develops slowly, not as sudden mortality

question_text: Match the following blood and tissue parasites of livestock in Column A with the vectors that transmit them in Column B.
explanation: Theileria annulata is transmitted by Hyalomma ticks; Babesia bigemina by Boophilus (now Rhipicephalus) ticks; Trypanosoma evansi (causing Surra) by Tabanid biting flies; and Anaplasma marginale by Dermacentor and other ticks. Knowing the correct vector is essential for control by acaricides or insect-net management.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

elimination_hints.hint: Theileria is tick-borne, not fly-borne
Theileria is not transmitted by Dermacentor
Babesia is transmitted by Boophilus, not Dermacentor

question_text: Match the following poultry diseases in Column A with their causal agents listed in Column B for layer and broiler farms.
explanation: Ranikhet disease is caused by Newcastle disease virus (a paramyxovirus); Marek's disease by Gallid herpesvirus 2; Infectious Bursal Disease by IBD virus of Birnaviridae; and Coccidiosis by Eimeria protozoan species. Knowing the exact pathogen guides correct vaccination and medication on the farm.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

elimination_hints.hint: Ranikhet is paramyxoviral, not IBD virus
Marek's is herpesviral, not Eimeria
Ranikhet is not protozoan

question_text: Match the following livestock zoonoses in Column A with the main route of transmission to humans listed in Column B.
explanation: Rabies is transmitted to humans by the bite of an infected animal; brucellosis spreads through unpasteurised milk and contact with aborted material; bovine TB through raw milk and aerosols; and anthrax through contact with infected carcasses, hides and spore-contaminated soil. Each route requires specific preventive measures.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

column_b.text: Contact with infected carcasses, hides, soil spores
Raw milk and aerosols from infected cattle
Unpasteurised milk and contact with aborted material
Bite of infected animal injecting saliva

elimination_hints.hint: Rabies is bite-transmitted, not via carcass
Brucellosis is via milk, not raw milk-aerosol of TB
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing routes of two milk-borne diseases

elimination_hints.hint: Rabies does not spread through aborted material
elimination_hints.misconception: Confusing reproductive with neural disease routes

question_text: Read the following assertion and reason about IBD vaccination in poultry carefully and choose the most correct option below.
explanation: Both statements are true and the reason correctly explains the assertion. IBD virus damages the bursa of Fabricius, the primary site of B-lymphocyte development in chicks. Hence chicks affected by IBD have impaired antibody production and respond poorly to other routine vaccines like Ranikhet, leading to vaccine failures. Good IBD control is therefore essential for the success of the entire poultry vaccination programme.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture
assertion: Chicks affected by IBD respond poorly to other routine vaccines such as Ranikhet vaccine.
reason: IBD virus destroys lymphocytes in the bursa of Fabricius and causes immunosuppression in chicks.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

elimination_hints.hint: Bursal damage is the very reason for immunosuppression and poor vaccine response
Bursal damage by IBD is well established
Poor vaccine response in IBD-affected chicks is well known

question_text: Read the assertion and reason about Surra disease control in camels and horses and choose the option which best fits.
explanation: The assertion is true but the reason is false. Surra is controlled mainly by drug therapy (quinapyramine, isometamidium) in affected animals and by reducing biting-fly populations through swamp drainage and fly traps. There is no widely available, effective routine vaccine against Trypanosoma evansi for livestock, hence vaccination is not the cornerstone of Surra control.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture
assertion: Surra in camels and horses is mainly controlled by drug therapy and biting-fly population reduction.
reason: An effective routine vaccine against Trypanosoma evansi is widely available and is the mainstay of Surra control.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

elimination_hints.hint: No routine effective Surra vaccine exists
The reason is biologically false
Drug therapy and vector control for Surra are well established

question_text: Read the assertion and reason about all-in all-out poultry management and biosecurity carefully and choose the option below.
explanation: Both statements are true and the reason correctly explains the assertion. By emptying the entire shed and applying a sanitary cleaning, disinfection and rest period before the next batch enters, the load of carry-over pathogens such as IBD virus, coccidial oocysts and Marek's virus on litter and surfaces is greatly reduced. This breaks the chain of transmission between successive flocks.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture
assertion: All-in all-out management with cleaning and rest period reduces carry-over pathogens in poultry sheds.
reason: Pathogen load on litter, equipment and surfaces is greatly reduced before the next batch enters the shed.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true

elimination_hints.hint: Reduced pathogen load is the very mechanism of all-in all-out
Pathogen reduction by cleaning and rest is well established
Reduced disease carry-over by all-in all-out is well documented

question_text: Arrange the following stages of the Fasciola gigantica life cycle in cattle in their correct chronological order from beginning.
explanation: Fasciola eggs in faeces hatch in water as miracidia, which infect freshwater snails of the genus Lymnaea. Inside the snail they multiply and emerge as cercariae, which encyst on grass blades as metacercariae. Grazing cattle ingest these metacercariae, which migrate through the gut and reach the liver, where they mature into adult flukes that produce more eggs.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

items.text: Cattle ingest metacercariae and adults develop in liver
Eggs in faeces hatch in water as miracidia
Miracidia infect Lymnaea snail and multiply
Cercariae emerge from snail and encyst as metacercariae on grass

options.text: Q → R → S → P
P → Q → R → S
S → R → Q → P
R → P → Q → S

elimination_hints.hint: Cattle ingestion cannot precede egg hatching
Cercariae cannot precede miracidia
Snail infection cannot be the first stage

question_text: Arrange the following standard poultry vaccination schedule events for broiler chicks in correct chronological order from day-old.
explanation: Broiler chicks are vaccinated against Marek's disease at the hatchery on day one. Ranikhet disease F1/LaSota is given around day 5-7. Infectious Bursal Disease vaccine is given around day 14, with a booster around day 21. Ranikhet booster (LaSota) is then given around day 28 to maintain protective immunity through the broiler cycle.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

items.text: Marek's disease vaccine on day 1 at hatchery
Ranikhet F1 vaccine around day 5 to 7
IBD vaccine around day 14 with booster around day 21

options.text: Q → R → S → P
P → S → R → Q
S → Q → P → R
R → P → Q → S

elimination_hints.hint: Day 28 booster cannot be the first vaccination
Day 14 IBD cannot precede day 1 Marek's
Day 5-7 vaccine cannot precede day 1 vaccine

question_text: State whether the following statement about coccidiosis prevention in commercial broilers is TRUE or FALSE with appropriate justification.
explanation: The statement is TRUE. Anticoccidial drugs (coccidiostats) such as monensin and salinomycin are routinely added to broiler starter and finisher feeds to suppress Eimeria multiplication in the gut. Live attenuated coccidiosis vaccines are increasingly used in breeders and as part of antimicrobial-stewardship programmes to reduce drug use in commercial flocks.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture
statement: Coccidiosis in broilers is commonly prevented by including anticoccidial drugs in starter and finisher feeds.

options.text: TRUE — coccidiostats in feed suppress Eimeria multiplication in the gut
FALSE — coccidiosis is prevented only by deworming injections
FALSE — coccidiosis is mainly prevented by Marek's vaccine
FALSE — coccidiosis only affects ducks not chickens

elimination_hints.hint: Coccidiosis is protozoan, not a worm needing dewormer
Marek's vaccine is for herpesvirus, not Eimeria
Coccidiosis severely affects chickens worldwide

question_text: Study the comparison table of major parasitic and viral diseases of livestock in the diagram and answer the question that follows.
explanation: The diagram shows distinct vector-pathogen-sign combinations for important livestock diseases. Theileriosis, caused by Theileria annulata, is transmitted specifically by Hyalomma ticks and presents with high fever, lymph node swelling and anaemia in cross-bred cattle. Other rows similarly distinguish babesiosis, Surra, fascioliasis and three viral poultry diseases by their respective agents and modes of spread.
correct_answer_text: Theileriosis is caused by Theileria annulata and transmitted by Hyalomma ticks.
topic: Animal Disease Management, Fisheries and Livestock Products
subject: agriculture

options.text: Theileriosis is caused by a virus and transmitted by mosquitoes
Theileriosis is caused by Theileria annulata and transmitted by Hyalomma ticks
Marek's disease is caused by Eimeria protozoan in young chicks only
Surra is transmitted by snails as the intermediate host of livestock

elimination_hints.hint: Theileria is a protozoan, not a virus
Marek's is herpesviral, not protozoan
Surra is fly-borne, not snail-borne
